  set; pre-A6 callers continue to use ``settings.model``.

  Implementation:
  - ``core/config/_settings.py`` Рђћ 3 new ``Field`` declarations with
    ``AliasChoices`` (matches the existing ``cognitive_reflection_model``
    pattern).
  - ``core/config/__init__.py`` Рђћ 3 new ``_TOML_TO_SETTINGS`` mappings.
  - ``core/agent/loop/agent_loop.py`` Рђћ ``AgenticLoop.__init__`` reads
    ``settings.act_model`` when ``model`` is None; ``_call_llm`` exposes
    optional ``model: str | None = None`` keyword override that threads
    through to ``self._adapter.agentic_call(model=...)`` and the
    ``build_adapter_request`` bridge path.
  - ``core/agent/loop/_decomposition.py`` Рђћ ``try_decompose`` reads
    ``settings.plan_model`` for ``GoalDecomposer(model=...)``.
  - ``core/agent/verify.py`` Рђћ ``_verify_llm_judge`` no longer stubs out.
    Builds a strict-JSON judge prompt, calls ``loop._call_llm(model=
    settings.judge_model)``, parses the response via
    ``_parse_judge_payload`` (tolerates code fences + bad JSON + non-
    numeric scores via clamp-and-default), surfaces failed reason as a
    ``judge_fail`` rubric_miss + reflexion_hint. New
    ``verify_turn_async`` + ``_verify_llm_judge_async`` pair lets
    ``finalize_and_return_async`` await the judge call under the same
    event loop instead of hopping through a thread pool. The sync
    wrapper retains a thread-pool fallback for sync callers. The judge
    call is bounded by a 120s ``asyncio.wait_for`` timeout, and the
    response's token usage is fed through ``loop._track_usage_async``
    so judge cost surfaces in the session TokenTracker (Codex MCP
    MEDIUM #4 fix; per-phase ``phase="judge"`` tagging deferred). 4
    fallback paths to rule-based with ``effective_mode=RULE_BASED``:
    loop ref is None, response is None, empty response text, LLM call
    raises (TimeoutError or otherwise).
  - ``core/agent/loop/_lifecycle.py`` Рђћ new ``_run_turn_verify_async``
    + shared ``_finalize_verify_outcome`` helper. ``finalize_and_return_async``
    awaits the async verify path (Codex MCP HIGH #2 fix); sync
    ``finalize_and_return`` still uses the sync wrapper for legacy callers.
  - ``core/agent/loop/_model_switching.py`` Рђћ drift-sync target reads
    ``settings.act_model or settings.model`` so an Opus-primary + Sonnet-
    act split doesn't revert mid-session (Codex MCP HIGH #1 fix).
  - ``tests/test_model_split.py`` (NEW, 22 tests) Рђћ settings defaults +
    env override + TOML mapping; ``AgenticLoop.__init__`` act_model
    cascade + explicit-model precedence + empty-fallback; ``_call_llm``
    signature contract; goal decomposition uses ``plan_model``;
    ``_verify_llm_judge`` actual LLM call + judge_fail path + 4 fallback
    cases (no loop / exception / None response / no judge_model);
    ``_judge_prompt`` truncation; ``_parse_judge_payload`` 5 edge cases.

  Frontier alignment (Socratic Q5): ReWOO (arxiv 2305.18323, 5x token
  efficiency via plan/observation decouple) + Claude Code Plan/Edit mode
  + Self-Discover (arxiv 2402.03620, task-level plan composition).

- **PR-CL-A3 Рђћ In-loop Verify (Reflexion-style verbal RL) + sessions
  DB persistence**. Per-turn verification of agent action quality,
  fired at the ``TURN_COMPLETED`` hook boundary so it doesn't interrupt
  mid-turn execution. Outcome is recorded into both :class:`SessionMetrics`
  (Tier 2 in-process aggregator) **and the ``sessions`` SQLite row**
  (durable across process restarts) so PR-CL-A1 (Dynamic Replan) can
  read it from either source; on verify FAIL the synthesised
  ``<reflexion>...</reflexion>`` block is consumed by the *next* ``arun``
  and prepended to the system prompt (verbal RL, Reflexion paper NeurIPS
  2023). The PR also closes the PR-CL-BUDGET handoff DB wiring gap Рђћ
  ``_check_session_budget_and_maybe_handoff`` now calls ``request_handoff``
  so the sessions row's ``handoff_state`` actually transitions to
  ``pending``.

  - ``core/agent/verify.py`` (NEW) Рђћ :class:`VerifyMode` StrEnum
    (``off`` / ``rule_based`` (default) / ``llm_judge``) +
    :class:`VerifyResult` frozen dataclass + ``verify_turn`` dispatcher
    + ``_verify_rule_based`` (structural checks: ``empty_turn`` /
    ``short_output`` / ``tool_error`` / ``model_action_required``) +
    ``synthesize_reflexion_hint`` (verbal-RL block renderer) +
    ``_verify_llm_judge`` (**stub** Рђћ falls back to rule_based and
    surfaces ``effective_mode=RULE_BASED`` in the payload; the real
    judge LLM call lands in PR-CL-A6 with the judge-model knob).
    Adds ``should_retry`` machine-readable signal (Codex MCP MEDIUM #4)
    Рђћ recoverable misses (``empty_turn`` / ``short_output`` / ``tool_error``)
    flip True; ``model_action_required`` alone keeps it False so PR-CL-A1
    doesn't loop on an operator-action item.
  - ``core/observability/session_metrics.py`` Рђћ extended ``SessionMetrics``
    with ``verify_pass_count`` / ``verify_fail_count`` /
    ``last_verify_passed`` / ``last_verify_mode`` /
    ``last_verify_rubric_misses`` / ``last_verify_reflexion_hint`` +
    new ``record_verify()`` mutator. ``to_session_row`` exposes all 5
    telemetry keys.
  - ``core/hooks/system.py`` Рђћ added ``TURN_VERIFY_PASSED`` /
    ``TURN_VERIFY_FAILED`` events (distinct from the pipeline-level
    ``VERIFICATION_PASS`` / ``VERIFICATION_FAIL`` pair which covers
    node-level guardrails). ``HookEvent`` total 72 Рєњ 74.
  - ``core/agent/loop/_lifecycle.py`` Рђћ new ``_run_turn_verify`` helper
    dispatched from both ``finalize_and_return`` (sync) and
    ``finalize_and_return_async`` (async) after the ``TURN_COMPLETED``
    hook fires.
  - ``core/agent/loop/agent_loop.py`` Рђћ ``arun`` reads + clears the
    Reflexion hint via ``_consume_reflexion_hint`` and prepends to
    ``system_prompt`` for the current attempt.
  - ``core/memory/session_manager.py`` Рђћ ``sessions`` table extended
    with 7 verify columns (``verify_pass_count`` / ``verify_fail_count`` /
    ``last_verify_passed`` / ``last_verify_mode`` /
    ``last_verify_effective_mode`` / ``last_verify_rubric_misses`` /
    ``last_verify_should_retry``) + ``upsert_verify_state()`` mutator
    + ``get_verify_state()`` reader. Idempotent ALTER TABLE migration
    inside the existing ``BEGIN IMMEDIATE`` transaction handles both
    fresh DBs and pre-A3 legacy DBs.
  - ``core/agent/loop/_lifecycle.py`` Рђћ ``_persist_verify_state`` mirror
    function writes Tier 2 SessionMetrics state to the ``sessions``
    row after every TURN_COMPLETED. Enriched ``TURN_VERIFY_*`` hook
    payload (Codex MCP LOW #8) includes ``session_id`` / ``rounds`` /
    ``termination_reason`` / ``tool_call_count`` so external renderers
    have full turn context.
  - ``core/agent/loop/agent_loop.py`` Рђћ ``_persist_handoff_request``
    closes the PR-CL-BUDGET DB wiring gap. The 2h-cap T-10min trigger
    in ``_check_session_budget_and_maybe_handoff`` now actually calls
    ``request_handoff`` so the sessions row transitions to ``pending``
    (was schema-only / unwired before).
    ``_sync_model_and_rebuild_prompt`` re-applies the reflexion hint
    on model drift / prompt-dirty rebuild (Codex MCP MEDIUM #6 Рђћ was
    silently dropping the hint).
  - ``core/wiring/bootstrap.py`` Рђћ default audit handlers registered
    for ``TURN_VERIFY_PASSED`` / ``TURN_VERIFY_FAILED`` (Codex MCP
    MEDIUM #7) matching the existing ``VERIFICATION_FAIL`` pattern.
  - ``tests/test_verify.py`` (NEW, 38 tests) Рђћ mode StrEnum, dataclass
    shape, env knob parsing, rule-based catches (4 reason codes +
    multi-miss combination), reflexion-hint synthesis, mode dispatch,
    SessionMetrics integration, hint consume+clear, exception-safe
    dispatch, ``should_retry`` allowlist semantics, DB persistence
    round-trip, legacy DB migration adds verify cols, end-to-end
    lifecycle Рєњ DB write, and handoff DB wiring (PR-CL-BUDGET closure).
  - 4 hook-count tests bumped 72 Рєњ 74.

  Three operator-tunable env knobs:
  - ``GEODE_VERIFY_MODE`` Рђћ ``off`` / ``rule_based`` / ``llm_judge``
  - ``GEODE_VERIFY_MIN_TEXT_CHARS`` Рђћ short-output threshold (default 10)

  Frontier alignment (Socratic Q5): Reflexion paper (NeurIPS 2023, 91%
  HumanEval pass@1 via verbal RL) + OpenAI o1 chain-of-verify + AgentHub
  per-branch verify gate + Claude Code Plan/Edit implicit verify.

### Changed

- **Scaffold consolidation Рђћ `CLAUDE.md` + `GEODE.md` ВаЋвЈѕ.** Absorbed the
  free-standing `### DONT Рђћ Real Incidents` table into the structured
  `### CANNOT` + `### Wiring Verification` + `### Refactoring Deception
  Prevention` tables so every prohibition rule lives in one indexable
  place with the originating sprint incident attached. Five new CANNOT
  rows distilled from prior incidents (graceful-contract scope / latest
  vs promoted SoT / dual-SoT drift invariant / ВаюЖ▒░ disambiguation /
  no-emoji-no-card UI). Consolidated the triple-listed Quality Gates
  recipe (was duplicated across ┬Д3 Implement, ┬Д4b Correctness, and the
  trailing `### Quality Gates` table) into a single trailing SoT with
  the two earlier locations now linking back. Compressed `Refactoring
  Deception Prevention` (5 rows Рєњ 2: implementation-completeness +
  CHANGELOG/PR-body parity) and `┬Д4c Cleanliness` (4-row table Рєњ one
  line delegating to the `anti-deception-checklist` skill) so the
  scaffold no longer duplicates skill content. Added cross-reference
  banners on `### CANNOT`, `### Wiring Verification`, the worktree row,
  the PR row, and the `4-layer stack` line so each scaffold table now
  names the skill or sibling document it pairs with.
- **`GEODE.md` runtime-vs-dev scope disambiguation.** Renamed
  `## CANNOT` Рєњ `## RUNTIME CANNOT` so the runtime agent-guardrail
  table is no longer confusable with the scaffold's development-time
  `### CANNOT`. Both headers now carry a one-line banner pointing at
  the sibling document.

### Fixed

- **G1 identity context Рђћ `RUNTIME CANNOT` header parity.**
  `core/agent/system_prompt.py:_build_identity_context` looked for the
  hardcoded literal `"## CANNOT"` when extracting the agent-identity
  block from `GEODE.md`. The scaffold-consolidation rename to
  `## RUNTIME CANNOT` would have silently dropped the 4-line CANNOT
  section (G3 Grounding / cross-validation / Confidence loopback /
  sub-agent delegation) from every LLM system prompt's
  `<agent_identity>` block. `target_sections` (line 477) and the
  surrounding module + function docstrings now reference
  `## RUNTIME CANNOT`. Verified by direct call Рђћ the block still
  contains all 4 RUNTIME CANNOT bullets after the rename.

## [0.99.44] - 2026-05-23

### Added

- **Follow-up F Рђћ GLM adapter family.** Two new built-ins land the
  ZhipuAI provider on the v0.99.39 :class:`LLMAdapter` registry:

  - ``glm-payg`` (``provider=glm``, ``source=payg``,
    ``billing_type=api``) Рђћ calls ``api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`` with
    ``settings.zai_api_key``. PAYG metered.
  - ``glm-coding-plan`` (``provider=glm``, ``source=subscription``,
    ``billing_type=subscription``) Рђћ calls
    ``api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4`` with the API key bound to a
    Coding Plan profile resolved via
    :func:`core.llm.routing.plan_registry.resolve_routing`. Refuses to
    fall back to PAYG when no Plan is registered Рђћ the picker source
    ``subscription`` means subscription.

  Both share :func:`build_async_openai_client` (per-adapter fresh
  client, no module-level singleton shadowing) and route
  ``tool_choice`` through ``normalize("glm", ...)`` for the Chat
  Completions nested ``function`` wire shape.

- **Follow-up A2 Рђћ OpenAI + Codex adapter route closure.** Removes the
  ``provider == "anthropic"`` guard from
  ``AgenticLoop.__init__`` so concrete-source resolution now works for
  every provider via :func:`core.llm.adapters.resolve_for`. The two
  Codex MCP findings from PR #1519 are closed:

  - **BLOCKER 2 (multi-turn translation).**
    ``core/llm/adapters/_openai_common.py`` gained ports of
    ``_convert_messages_to_openai`` / ``_convert_messages_to_responses``
    from the legacy provider Рђћ assistant ``tool_use`` blocks re-encode
    into Chat ``tool_calls`` (nested ``function``) or Codex Responses
    ``function_call`` typed items; user ``tool_result`` blocks become
    ``role: tool`` follow-ups with ``tool_call_id`` (Chat) or
    ``function_call_output`` items with ``call_id`` (Codex). Pre-A2 the
    adapter passed the Anthropic content list through verbatim and the
    SDK returned 400.

  - **HIGH 1 (Codex encrypted-reasoning replay).**
    :class:`core.llm.adapters.AdapterCallResult` gained
    ``reasoning_items`` + ``reasoning_summaries`` fields.
    ``CodexOAuthAdapter.acomplete`` captures ``type: reasoning`` typed
    items from the ``response.output_item.done`` SSE stream and
    surfaces them on the result. ``_legacy_bridge.build_adapter_request``
    pulls ``codex_reasoning_items`` off the prior assistant turn and
    threads them via ``AdapterCallRequest.provider_options``;
    ``_build_codex_call_kwargs`` calls
    :func:`core.llm.adapters._openai_common.inject_reasoning_replay`
    to prepend the encrypted_content blobs into the next-turn ``input``.
    Without this chain gpt-5.x ``store=False`` models lose their chain
    of thought across turns.

  - Tool_choice translation now mirrors the Anthropic adapter pattern.
    OpenAI Chat receives ``auto``/``none``/``required`` (the
    adapter-neutral ``"any"`` maps to ``"required"``); Codex Responses
    same mapping, with unknown literals defaulting to ``"auto"``.

  Tests: ``tests/core/llm/adapters/test_openai_multi_turn.py`` (14
  cases, Chat + Responses converters + reasoning replay) +
  ``test_codex_reasoning_replay.py`` (12 cases Рђћ SSE accumulation Рєњ
  result Рєњ bridge Рєњ next-turn provider_options chain).

- **PR-CL-BUDGET Рђћ 2-hour wall-clock cap + automatic T-10min hand-off**.
  Foundation PR for the Cognitive Loop sprint (A3 Рєњ A6 Рєњ A1). Replaces
  the per-AgenticLoop turn hard-cap with a session-wide wall-clock
  budget (default 7200s) and an automatic graceful exit at T-600s
  remaining.

  - ``core/agent/budget.py`` (NEW) Рђћ ``TimeBudget`` config dataclass +
    ``start_session_budget()`` / ``check_session_budget()`` /
    ``budget_summary()`` helpers. Budget *state* (start time, latch flag)
    lives on the existing :class:`SessionMetrics` so the budget travels
    with the ContextVar across nested loops. Defaults pulled from
    ``GEODE_SESSION_TIME_BUDGET_S`` env knob.
  - ``core/agent/handoff.py`` (NEW) Рђћ DB-backed state machine adapted
    from hermes-agent ``hermes_state.py:218-220`` + ``gateway/run.py:
    3712-3766`` watcher pattern. 5 states (NONE / PENDING / RUNNING /
    COMPLETED / FAILED) with atomic CAS helpers
    (``request_handoff`` / ``claim_handoff`` / ``complete_handoff`` /
    ``fail_handoff`` / ``get_handoff`` / ``list_pending_handoffs``).
    GEODE adapts the trigger from hermes's user-initiated
    ``/handoff <platform>`` slash command to an *automatic* wall-clock
    crossing (operator decision in
    ``project_budget_handoff_decision`` memory).
  - ``core/observability/session_metrics.py`` Рђћ extended ``SessionMetrics``
    with 4 new fields (``time_budget_start_s`` / ``time_budget_total_s``
    / ``handoff_threshold_s`` / ``handoff_triggered_at``) + 3 methods
    (``start_time_budget`` / ``time_budget_remaining_s`` /
    ``is_handoff_due``). ``is_handoff_due`` is **one-shot** Рђћ first
    crossing latches ``handoff_triggered_at`` so the AgenticLoop fires
    HANDOFF_TRIGGERED exactly once per session, not every round.
  - ``core/memory/session_manager.py`` Рђћ schema extended with 4 columns
    on the ``sessions`` table (``handoff_state TEXT`` /
    ``handoff_platform TEXT`` / ``handoff_error TEXT`` /
    ``handoff_triggered_at REAL``) + a partial index
    (``idx_sessions_handoff_state``) for the watcher poll. Existing DBs
    migrate via additive ALTER TABLE inside ``__init__`` Рђћ
    ``PRAGMA table_info`` is the SoT for column presence (idempotent on
    fresh + migrated DBs).
  - ``core/hooks/system.py`` Рђћ added ``HANDOFF_TRIGGERED`` /
    ``HANDOFF_COMPLETED`` / ``HANDOFF_FAILED`` events. Payload schema:
    ``{session_id, platform, remaining_s, budget_total_s,
    handoff_threshold_s, handoff_triggered_at, ts}``.
  - ``core/agent/loop/agent_loop.py`` Рђћ ``_check_round_guards`` extended
    to call the new ``_check_session_budget_and_maybe_handoff`` helper
    after the existing ``round_limit`` / ``time_budget`` gates. The
    ``arun`` entry now calls ``_maybe_start_session_budget`` once per
    session (idempotent guard via ``time_budget_total_s > 0``). ``getattr``
    guard preserves the ``_StubLoop`` test pattern from
    ``tests/test_arun_round_guards.py``.
  - ``core/channels/binding.py`` + ``core/cli/typer_serve.py`` Рђћ
    ``gateway_max_turns`` default flipped from ``20`` to ``0`` (unlimited).
    The session-wide 2h wall-clock budget is now the global safety net;
    ``gateway_time_budget_s`` (120s per-message default) remains the
    per-binding cap.
  - ``tests/test_budget.py`` (NEW, 14 tests) Рђћ defaults, dataclass shape,
    populate + check, one-shot handoff latch, hard expiry, ContextVar
    isolation, explicit-target arg, ``to_session_row`` row shape.
  - ``tests/test_handoff.py`` (NEW, 15 tests) Рђћ 5-state machine atomic
    CAS verification, get/list contract, ``handoff_summary`` rendering,
    legacy-DB migration adds columns, idempotent re-open.

  Frontier alignment (4 systems, Socratic Q5):
  - **Claude Code** ``agent-loop.ts:checkBudgetExhausted`` Рђћ wall-clock
    + token budget per-turn boundary.
  - **Codex CLI** ``--budget-seconds`` flag Рђћ single wall-clock knob.
  - **OpenClaw** Lane TTL Рђћ per-lane time cap inside the gateway.
  - **Hermes Agent** ``sessions.handoff_state`` 3-col DB state machine
    + ``_handoff_watcher`` atomic-claim asyncio task.

  Out of scope (separate PRs): asyncio ``_handoff_watcher`` background
  task in ``typer_serve``'s ``_serve_loop`` (this PR lays the schema +
  CAS helpers; the actual watcher follows once a consumer is wired).
  Successor re-spawn from a hand-off record (the watcher logs + the
  user manually invokes ``geode resume <session_id>`` for now). See
  follow-up tasks in ``project_budget_handoff_decision`` memory.

## [0.99.43] - 2026-05-23

### Added

- **PR-CSP-14 Рђћ Loop 3 (literature paper-analysis) backend + bundle
  serving infrastructure**. Final loop of the 3-loop port from
  open-coscientist (`nodes/literature_review.py:840-873`). Per
  `docs/plans/2026-05-23-seed-gen-loop3-bundle-serving.md` (Phase 2
  SoT doc).

  - `core/tools/literature_snapshot.py` Рђћ NEW `FreezePaperSnapshotTool`.
    Per-paper snapshot writer with content-hash deterministic cache,
    arxiv_id pattern validation, env-anchored containment under
    `docs/petri-bundle/literature/`, atomic tmp+rename, uuid nonce
    on the file name to dodge same-second concurrent collisions.
  - `plugins/seed_generation/literature_snapshot.py` Рђћ read-side
    helpers (`load_snapshot`, `iter_snapshots`).
  - `plugins/seed_generation/agents/literature_review.py` + `.md` Рђћ
    NEW `LiteratureReview` agent. Dispatches one sub-agent walking
    the 4-phase pipeline (query_gen Рєњ paper_fetch Рєњ per-paper analysis
    Рєњ synthesis). `max_papers=0` (manifest default) short-circuits
    to no-op for byte-equivalent back-compat with pre-CSP-14 runs.
  - Orchestrator: `_PHASE_ORDER` inserts `literature_review` after
    `supervisor`, before `generator`. NEW state fields
    `articles_with_reasoning: str` + `literature_snapshots: dict`.
    Both Generator + Critic + Evolver inject the literature block as
    a prompt prefix when populated (Codex MCP MEDIUM fix-up Рђћ original
    diff only wired Generator).
  - `scripts/build_literature_listing.py` Рђћ NEW build step. Scans
    snapshots + cross-refs `mutations.jsonl` evidence + seed frontmatter
    `references:` for the `cited_by` reverse index. Defensive parser
    handles both typed-array (post petri-autoresearch realign) and
    flat (pre-realign) evidence shapes.
  - `.github/workflows/pages.yml` Рђћ gains a `Build literature
    listing.json` step before the Next.js export.
  - `scripts/validate_petri_bundle.py` Рђћ extended to count literature
    snapshots alongside audit archives in the OK line.
  - Manifest + config: `SeedRoleSpec.max_papers` (0 or [1, 20]) +
    `queries_per_run` ([1, 10]) validators. Operator override slots
    in `SelfImprovingLoopBindings`.
  - **Seed bundle_sync** (added 2026-05-23 after mockup feedback Рђћ
    discovered the publish hierarchy gap):
    `plugins/seed_generation/bundle_sync.py` mirrors the audit-side
    `sync_eval_to_bundle` pattern. `sync_run_to_bundle(run_dir)` runs
    on `Pipeline.arun` finalization and selectively copies
    `state/seed-generation/<run_id>/{state.json, survivors.json,
    meta_review.json, candidates/<survivor_id>.md}` into
    `docs/petri-bundle/seeds/<run_id>/`. Drafts that didn't survive
    stay in `state/` only Рђћ the bundle is a publish surface, not an
    archive. `.gitignore` carries `!docs/petri-bundle/seeds/**` so
    the synced files actually enter git (parity with the audit-side
    `!docs/petri-bundle/logs/**` rule; avoids the PR-G5b #1350
    silent-drop anti-pattern). NEW `scripts/build_seeds_listing.py`
    aggregates run rows into `docs/petri-bundle/seeds/listing.json`
    for the bundle-UI consumers; wired into `pages.yml` next to the
    literature build step. `validate_petri_bundle.py` extended to
    count synced runs alongside audit archives + literature snapshots.
    The CSP-14-UI mockups read these surfaces (`bundle-landing.html` +
    `seeds.html` reference the listing.json + per-run state.json).
    Same gitignore exception added for `docs/petri-bundle/literature/`
    Рђћ the writer's GEODE_REPO_ROOT-anchored path was correct but the
    new dir wasn't covered by the existing audit-logs exception, so
    git tracking would have silently dropped fresh snapshots.

  **Bundle UI (Next.js literature index + detail pages + inline
  reference card in audit log viewer)** is deferred to a follow-up PR
  Рђћ the backend wiring (agent + tool + snapshots on disk + build
  step + listing.json) is complete; the visual surfaces (steps 7+8
  of the 13-step plan) land in PR-CSP-14-UI.

  **Production wire-up dependency**: `cli.py:_dispatch_pipeline()`
  still creates an empty `PipelineRegistry` (S11 not landed for any
  role yet Рђћ same gap as Phase 1 PR #1504's `num_turns` knob). The
  `max_papers` override is fully validated end-to-end via the test
  fixtures + Codex MCP review; production CLI flow waits on the S11
  registry wire-up which covers all roles uniformly.

  Codex MCP review (thread `019e5260-1c30-78c0-969b-61f0f658c8c2`)
  caught 2 MEDIUM + 2 LOW pre-push; addressed inline:
  - MEDIUM: Critic + Evolver inject `articles_with_reasoning` (not
    just Generator). MEDIUM #1 closed.
  - MEDIUM: uuid nonce on snapshot file name dodges same-second
    concurrent write collisions. MEDIUM #2 closed.
  - LOW: this CHANGELOG entry.
  - LOW (acknowledged): `cost_preview.py` accounting gets a phantom
    one-run estimate for `literature_review` when `max_papers=0`;
    follow-up to add a phase-skip bypass.

### Changed

- **Plan docs sync with PR-SESSION-METRICS rename**. 3 plan docs updated
  to reflect ``journal.jsonl`` Рєњ ``transcript.jsonl`` rename + ``SessionJournal``
  Рєњ ``SessionTranscript`` alias-shim absorption from PR #1531:
  - ``docs/plans/2026-05-19-self-improving-loop-wiring-sprint.md`` Рђћ
    schema row #7 ВЮў ``journal.jsonl`` Рєњ ``transcript.jsonl`` + Phase C
    verification checklist ВЮў path Ж░▒ВІа
  - ``docs/plans/2026-05-21-self-improving-loop-ux.md`` Рђћ UX mockup ВЮў
    "G9 Рђћ last 9 journal events" Рєњ "transcript events" + Stage РЉБ telemetry
    ВЮў ``SessionJournal event`` Рєњ ``SessionTranscript lifecycle event``
    (3 places)
  - ``docs/plans/2026-05-23-sil-5theme-closure.md`` Рђћ C4 row ВЌљ PR #1531
    sidecar ьЮАВѕў + race-surface 0 ьЏёВєЇ вЁИьіИ ВХћЖ░ђ

  Codex MCP review (thread ``019e53a5-c793-7d13-b40c-562fb28b0d9f``): 4 checks
  pass + 1 LOW finding (UX residual 392/412 line) addressed in this commit.

## [0.99.42] - 2026-05-23

> Observability central-isation release. ``SessionMetrics`` ВІаВёц (Tier 2 of
> 3-Tier preservation), ``SessionJournal`` Рєњ ``SessionTranscript`` alias-
> shim ьЮАВѕў, ``journal/`` directory depth ВХЋВєї. PR-SIL-5THEME C4 ВЮў sidecar
> race Ж░ђ SessionMetrics ContextVar вІеВЮ╝ Ж░ЮВ▓┤вАю ьЮАВѕўвљўвЕ░ ВўЂЖхг ьЋ┤Вєї. +15
> tests (6834 Рєњ 6849).

### Added

- **`SessionMetrics` Рђћ session-scoped state aggregator (Tier 2 of 3-Tier
  preservation architecture)**. Centralises ~13 scattered session-scoped
  state (LLM cost / tool calls / provider routing / failover counts /
  mutation lifecycle / Goodhart surface) into a single ContextVar-bound
  dataclass at ``core/observability/session_metrics.py``.

  **Frontier ВаЋваг** Рђћ Claude Code ``AgentLoopState.totalUsage`` +
  Hermes ``sessions`` SQLite table column shape + Paperclip ``usageJson``
  JSONB column schema ВЮў cumulative aggregate grain ВЮ╝В╣ў. Hermes ВЮў
  ``_SESSION_ID: ContextVar`` family ВЎђ Ж░ЎВЮђ multi-session Ж▓Евдг ьїеьё┤.

  **C4 sidecar ьЮАВѕў** Рђћ PR-SIL-5THEME C4 ВЮў ``_LAST_LLM_CALL_USAGE``
  module-level dict + PR-C4.fix-contextvar ВЮў ``ContextVar[dict]`` +
  ``_UsageProxy`` shim ВаёвХђ SessionMetrics ВЮў ``last_call_input_tokens
  / last_call_output_tokens / last_call_elapsed_seconds / last_call_model``
  4-field snapshot Вю╝вАю ьЮАВѕў. propose() Ж░ђ Ж░ЎВЮђ ContextVar ВЋѕВЌљВёю
  ``current_session_metrics()`` ьўИВХювАю race Ж░ђвіЦВё▒ 0. PR #1530 ВЮў flaky
  test fail ьЋ┤Вєї.

  **API**:
  - ``current_session_metrics()`` Рђћ ContextVar lazy-init lookup
  - ``session_metrics_scope(session_id, gen_tag, component)`` Рђћ context
    manager binding (mirrors ``session_journal_scope``)
  - ``SessionMetrics.accumulate_llm_call(...)`` Рђћ per-call additive
  - ``SessionMetrics.increment_tool_call() / increment_mutation() /
    record_retry() / record_circuit_breaker_trip() / record_error() /
    record_rollback() / record_goodhart()`` Рђћ counter mutation
  - ``SessionMetrics.to_session_row()`` Рђћ sessions.jsonl persistence shape

  **15 new tests** (`tests/test_session_metrics.py`):
  defaults / accumulate additive + last-call overwrite / reset_last_call
  preserves cumulative / 5 counter APIs / persistence shape / ContextVar
  lifecycle (lazy / nested isolation / exception restore) / C4 integration.

### Changed

- **`SessionJournal` Рєњ `SessionTranscript` ьЮАВѕў, ``journal/`` directory
  depth ВаюЖ▒░**. Pre-existing 3-Tier preservation ВЋёьѓцьЁЇВ▓ўЖ░ђ
  ``core/runtime_state/transcript.py`` docstring ВЌљ ВЮ┤в»И ВаЋВЮўвЈ╝ ВъѕВЌѕВю╝вѓў
  ``SessionJournal`` (ВЮўвЈёвљю Tier 2 Въљвдг) Ж░ђ ВІцВаювАювіћ self-improving-loop
  ВЮў *в│ёвЈё Tier 1 event log* вАю Вџ┤ВўЂ ВцЉВЮ┤ВЌѕвІц Рђћ вЉљ Ж░ЮВ▓┤Ж░ђ schema /
  path / API Ж░ђ вІцвЦИ В▒ё Ж│хВА┤. в│И PR ВЮ┤ ВаЋьЋЕ ьџїв│х:

  - ``core/observability/session_journal.py`` Рєњ thin alias-shim Вю╝вАю ВХЋВєї
    (269 callsite в│ђЖ▓й 0). ``SessionJournal.append(event, payload=...)``
    Ж░ђ ``SessionTranscript.record_lifecycle_event(...)`` вАю delegating
  - ``SessionTranscript`` ВЌљ ``record_lifecycle_event`` вЕћВёювЊю ВІаВёц Рђћ
    free-form event + ``{ts, session_id, gen_tag, component, level,
    event, payload}`` ВіцьѓцвДѕвАю ЖИ░ВА┤ Journal ВѓгВџЕВъљ в│┤ВА┤
  - file ВЮ┤вдё ``journal.jsonl`` Рєњ ``transcript.jsonl`` (mass rename, 269
    reference)
  - вћћваЅьё░вдг depth ВХЋВєї: ``~/.geode/journal/transcripts/<slug>/<id>.jsonl``
    Рєњ ``~/.geode/transcripts/<slug>/<id>.jsonl``. ``GLOBAL_JOURNAL_DIR``
    ВЮ┤вдёВЮђ ``GLOBAL_TRANSCRIPTS_DIR`` ВЮў backward-compat alias ВюаВДђ
  - ``core/cli/cmd_lifecycle.py`` ВЮў disk usage label ``"journal"`` Рєњ
    ``"transcripts"`` ВаЋваг

  **3-Tier ВхюВбЁ**:

  ```
  Tier 1  SessionTranscript   (append-only event log) Рђћ transcripts/*.jsonl
  Tier 2  SessionMetrics      (cumulative aggregate) Рђћ sessions.jsonl row
  Tier 3  SessionCheckpoint   (pipeline state) Рђћ state.json
  ```

  Frontier вфЁвфЁ ВаЋваг Рђћ Claude Code ``transcript`` / OpenClaw
  ``transcript`` / Hermes ``messages`` ВЮў Tier-1 ВЮ╝В╣ў. ``journal`` ВЮў
  ВЮ╝в░ў вфЁВѓг вфеьўИВё▒ ьЋ┤Вєї.

## [0.99.41] - 2026-05-23

> SIL 5-theme closure release. Bench S6b production wiring + ADR-012 ┬ДS6/┬ДS6b
> amendment + P3 modality Ж░ђВцЉ вХёвдг + E1 mutation cost ledger + D4 X2
> telemetry + D1 PAYG exclusion enforcement. PR #1524 (6 commits + plan + fix
> up). +176 new tests (6658 Рєњ 6834).

### Changed

- **PR-SIL-5THEME C6 Рђћ D1 provider B/C closure (PAYG exclusion enforcement)**.
  Operator decision (durable memory `project_payg_exclusion_decision.md`,
  2026-05-23) Вю╝вАю autoresearch / Petri audit ВЮў provider ВёаьЃЮВДђВЌљВёю
  PAYG (Anthropic API key ВДЂВаЉ Ж▓░Ваю, `api_key`) ВўЂЖхг exclude. ВъћВА┤ ВўхВЁў:
  `claude-cli` (Claude Code Max OAuth) / `openai-codex` (ChatGPT Plus
  OAuth) / `auto` (manifest cascade). ВъћВА┤ ВЮИьћёвЮ╝ ВйћвЊю (`auth.toml`,
  BillingError panel, `default_plan_for_payg`) віћ в│┤ВА┤.

  **`Source` literal narrowing**:
  `Literal["claude-cli", "openai-codex", "api_key", "auto"]` Рєњ
  `Literal["claude-cli", "openai-codex", "auto"]`. Operator Ж░ђ
  `~/.geode/config.toml` ВЌљ `source = "api_key"` вфЁВІю ВІю Pydantic
  ValidationError в░юьЎћ + Вќ┤віљ Ж░њВЮ┤ invalid ВЮИВДђ вфЁВІю (PR-C-P1 ВЮў
  silent-fallback В░евІе ьїеьё┤ ВъгВѓгВџЕ). вХёвдгвљю `MutatorConfig.source`
  (line 248 ВЮў в│ёвЈё Literal) ВЮђ `api_key` ьЈгьЋе 4-element ВюаВДђ Рђћ mutator
  LLM ьўИВХюВЮђ audit role Ж│╝ в│ёЖ░ю, operator Ж░ђ Anthropic API key вАю
  mutator Вџ┤ВўЂ ВъљВюа.

  **Default change**: `PetriRoleConfig.source` + `AutoresearchConfig.source`
  + `autoresearch/train.py:SOURCE` вфевЉљ default `"auto" Рєњ "claude-cli"`.
  `auto` віћ manifest cascade Ж░ђ silent PAYG fallback Ж░ђвіЦ (`fallback_to_payg`
  True ВЮ╝ вЋї) Рєњ вфЁВІю `claude-cli` default вЕ┤ leak 0. operator Ж░ђ explicit
  Вю╝вАю `"auto"` вўљвіћ `"openai-codex"` вфЁВІю ВІю ЖиИвїђвАю ВаЂВџЕ.

  **`check_subscription_cli_for_source` pre-flight** (ВІаЖию,
  `autoresearch/prepare.py`): `source` setting ВЮ┤ `claude-cli` вўљвіћ
  `openai-codex` ВЮ╝ вЋї ьЋ┤вІ╣ CLI binary ВЮў PATH Ж░ђВџЕВё▒ Ж▓ђВдЮ. вХђВъг ВІю
  actionable error (`GEODE_CLAUDE_CLI_BIN` / `GEODE_CODEX_CLI_BIN` env
  override hint ьЈгьЋе). `auto` source віћ cascade Ж░ђ fallback В▒ЁВъёВЮ┤вЮ╝
  pre-flight skip.

  **`_read_role_from_self_improving_loop` ВЮў ValidationError graceful
  fallback**: ЖИ░ВА┤ operator config Ж░ђ `source = "api_key"` Ж░ђВДё В▒ёвАю
  load ВІю ValidationError в░юьЎћ Рєњ petri standalone CLI ВЮў user_overrides
  read віћ graceful ьЋўЖ▓ї legacy `petri.toml` вАю fallback (`petri_role_legacy_fallback`
  event emit Вю╝вАю ВХћВаЂ Ж░ђвіЦ). autoresearch / mutator ВЮў ВДЂВаЉ ьўИВХюВЮђ
  ВЌгВаёьъѕ ValidationError Ж░ђ ВЮўвЈёвљю surface Рђћ вЉљ Ж▓йвАю ВЮў ВЮўвЈё вХёвдг.

  **Known-defaults filter expansion**: `_read_role_from_self_improving_loop`
  ВЮў source ьЋёьё░Ж░ђ `"auto"` ВЎИ `"claude-cli"` вЈё known-defaults set ВЌљ
  ВХћЖ░ђ Рђћ override output ВЮ┤ operator-explicit override вДї surface (default
  Ж░њВЮ┤ silent ьЋўЖ▓ї вЁИВХю ВЋѕ вље). explicit `"claude-cli"` ВёцВаЋВЮў silent corner
  віћ minor UX nit (ьџеЖ│╝ вЈЎВЮ╝).

  **Test guards** (`tests/test_d1_provider_closure.py` ВІаЖию, 14 tests):
  - `Source` literal Рђћ `api_key` Pydantic reject (PetriRoleConfig +
    AutoresearchConfig, 2 tests)
  - 3 valid sources (`claude-cli` / `openai-codex` / `auto`) ВѕўвЮй (3 tests)
  - Default `claude-cli` (PetriRoleConfig + AutoresearchConfig, 2 tests)
  - `check_subscription_cli_for_source` pre-flight: missing binary
    actionable error (claude-cli + openai-codex), env override wins,
    auto skip, unknown source graceful (5 tests)
  - 4-backend matrix: `api_key` reject + 3 valid accept (2 tests for
    both configs)

  **Existing test updates**:
  - `test_autoresearch_defaults_match_train_module` Рђћ expect `source ==
    "claude-cli"` (was `"auto"`)
  - `test_load_reads_autoresearch_subsection` Рђћ expect `source ==
    "claude-cli"` for untouched field
  - `test_petri_role_default_source_is_auto` Рєњ renamed
    `test_petri_role_default_source_is_subscription_first` Рђћ expect
    `claude-cli`
  - `test_set_source_updates_override` (petri CLI) Рђћ use `"openai-codex"`
    instead of `"api_key"` (latter rejected by Pydantic)
  - `test_read_role_override_prefers_self_improving_loop` Рђћ use
    `"openai-codex"` non-default source

  **Backward compat**: `Mutator` source literal (mutator LLM, distinct
  from audit roles) preserved with `api_key` Рђћ operator can still use
  Anthropic API for mutator. `credential_source.py` ВЮў PAYG fallback
  ВйћвЊю Ж▓йвАю в│┤ВА┤ (вІцвЦИ caller Ж░ђ вфЁВІю ьўИВХю ВІю ьЎюВё▒). ЖИ░ВА┤ `config.toml`
  ВЮў `api_key` ВёцВаЋ operator: petri standalone CLI віћ graceful (legacy
  fallback + telemetry event), autoresearch ВДЂВаЉ ьўИВХюВЮђ actionable
  ValidationError вАю migrate ВЋѕвѓ┤.

## [0.99.40] - 2026-05-23

### Added

- **Follow-up D Рђћ UI surface for the adapter registry.** Two new
  read-only Typer subcommands:

  - ``geode adapters list`` enumerates the 6 registered adapters with
    ``billing_type`` and per-adapter ``test_environment`` status.
  - ``geode adapters detect-model <name>`` reports the currently
    configured model + provenance via the adapter's
    ``detect_credential()`` hook.
  - ``geode audit-seeds config`` shows the 7-role ├Ќ (provider, model,
    source) binding matrix resolved by the picker, plus the judge
    panel voters.

  UI follows the ``feedback_no_box_ui_no_emoji`` rule: dense aligned
  table, plain text headers, no box-card / emoji decoration.

- **Follow-up C Рђћ S11 PipelineRegistry wire-up.** ``cli._dispatch_pipeline``
  now populates the ``PipelineRegistry`` with one concrete agent per
  ``manifest.enabled_roles`` (generator / critic / proximity / pilot /
  ranker / evolver / meta_reviewer) via the new
  ``plugins/seed_generation/_registry_builder.py`` helper. Each agent
  is instantiated with the picker binding's ``model`` + ``source``;
  Ranker additionally receives ``picker_result.voters`` so each judge
  follows its own binding. The pre-S11 placeholder
  (``registry = PipelineRegistry()`` with a runtime "no registered
  agent" error) is replaced Рђћ the production ``geode seeds run`` flow
  can now actually invoke the pipeline end-to-end. The shared
  ``SubAgentManager`` is built once via ``build_subagent_manager()``
  with best-effort AgentRegistry + tool handlers + ``IsolatedRunner``;
  observability hooks remain unattached (gateway runtime path is
  Follow-up D).

- **Follow-up B Рђћ Picker bindings propagation to SubTask.source.** Each
  seed-generation agent now passes ``source=self.adapter_source`` on
  ``SubTask`` construction so the picker-resolved per-role source
  reaches the spawned worker's ``AgenticLoop``. ``BaseSeedAgent`` gained
  an ``adapter_source`` property + ``picker_source_to_adapter_source``
  free function that translates the picker's historical source names
  (``api_key`` / ``claude-cli`` / ``openai-codex`` / ``auto``) into the
  paperclip-aligned adapter source values (``payg`` / ``subscription`` /
  ``adapter``) consumed by :func:`core.llm.adapters.resolve_for`. The
  Ranker agent uses ``picker_source_to_adapter_source(voter.source)``
  per voter so each judge follows its own binding. ``Pipeline`` gained
  a ``bindings: dict[str, Any]`` kwarg (default ``{}``) that
  ``cli._dispatch_pipeline`` populates from ``picker_result.bindings``
  Рђћ stored for observability / journaling but NOT re-injected at
  ``SubTask`` time (agents already carry the binding values).

  Closes the Codex MCP 2026-05-23 BLOCKER 1 finding ("the main
  seed-generation path appears unwired") from PR #1519: source now
  reaches the worker for every role's ``SubTask`` creation site
  (generator / critic / pilot / ranker / evolver / meta_reviewer /
  supervisor / proximity).

- **Follow-up A Рђћ AgenticLoop opt-in adapter route + source threading.**
  ``SubTask.source`` + ``WorkerRequest.source`` carry the picker-resolved
  adapter source (``payg`` / ``subscription`` / ``adapter``) through the
  parent Рєњ worker subprocess boundary into the spawned
  :class:`AgenticLoop`. When ``source`` is set AND ``provider="anthropic"``,
  ``AgenticLoop._call_llm`` routes through
  :func:`core.llm.adapters.resolve_for(provider, source)` Рєњ
  :meth:`LLMAdapter.acomplete` instead of the legacy
  ``resolve_agentic_adapter(provider)`` path. Translation lives in
  ``core/llm/adapters/_legacy_bridge.py``
  (``build_adapter_request`` / ``agentic_response_from_adapter_result``);
  ``AdapterCallRequest`` gained ``tool_choice`` + ``provider_options``
  fields to carry the loop-side knobs. Anthropic adapters translate the
  ``tool_choice`` value (``"auto"`` / ``"any"`` / ``"none"`` / ``"required"`` /
  dict) into Anthropic's ``{"type": ...}`` payload so the loop's wrap-up
  ``{"type": "none"}`` actually suppresses tool calls on the new route.
  Concrete source with a missing registry entry HARD-FAILS Рђћ silent
  fallback would mask operator misconfiguration (Codex MCP 2026-05-23
  HIGH 2). Non-Anthropic providers (``openai`` / ``openai-codex`` /
  ``glm``) thread ``source`` for observability but stay on the legacy
  adapter Рђћ full OpenAI / Codex multi-turn translation + Codex
  encrypted-reasoning replay lands as **Follow-up A2**. Legacy callers
  (empty ``source``) see no behaviour change.

- **LLM adapter abstraction (paperclip pattern) Рђћ Layer 4 Protocol +
  Layer 3 built-ins.** New :class:`core.llm.adapters.LLMAdapter`
  Protocol (paperclip ``ServerAdapterModule`` mirror) + mutable
  registry (``register_adapter`` / ``unregister_adapter`` /
  ``resolve_for(provider, source)`` / ``bootstrap_builtins``). Six
  built-in adapters covering all PAYG / Subscription / Adapter paths
  for Anthropic + OpenAI:

  | name | provider | source | billing_type |
  |---|---|---|---|
  | ``anthropic-payg`` | anthropic | payg | api |
  | ``anthropic-oauth`` | anthropic | subscription | subscription |
  | ``claude-cli`` | anthropic | adapter | subscription_included |
  | ``openai-payg`` | openai | payg | api |
  | ``codex-oauth`` | openai | subscription | subscription |
  | ``codex-cli`` | openai | adapter | subscription_included |

  Registry boot happens once at runtime construction via the
  ``build_llm_adapters`` wiring step. External plugins implement the
  Protocol and register via :func:`core.llm.adapters.register_adapter`
  from their entry point (paperclip's external-adapter pattern).

- **Picker Рєњ adapter resolution glue.** New
  :func:`plugins.seed_generation.picker.binding_to_adapter_source` +
  :func:`resolve_binding_to_adapter` collapse a :class:`RoleBinding`
  into a concrete :class:`LLMAdapter`. The picker's historical source
  strings (``api_key`` / ``claude-cli`` / ``openai-codex``) are
  translated to the adapter Protocol's ``payg`` / ``subscription`` /
  ``adapter`` names via a single SoT table Рђћ both naming schemes are
  accepted in ``[seed_generation.role.<role>]`` overrides.
- **PR-SIL-5THEME C5 Рђћ D4 X2 system-prompt model-identity injection telemetry**.
  `HookEvent.PROMPT_ASSEMBLED` Ж░ђ `core/hooks/system.py:69` ВЌљ ВаЋВЮўвљљ
  Вю╝вѓў fire 0ьџї ВўђвІц Рђћ X2 injection (Option B ВЮў вІеВЮ╝-line model
  identity statement, `core/agent/system_prompt.py:337`) ВЮ┤ вДц round
  вДѕвІц в░юьЎћьЋўВДђвДї Ж┤ђВИА marker 0. v0.52.5-style stale-ack ьџїЖиђ в░юВЃЮ ВІю
  production debug ьЋёВџћьќѕвІц. C5 Ж░ђ вЉљ wiring point ьЎюВё▒:

  **Wiring 1 Рђћ `_sync_model_and_rebuild_prompt` Ж░ђ PROMPT_ASSEMBLED в░юьЎћ**.
  System prompt rebuild ьЏё (drift detected OR prompt_dirty=True) hook
  в░юьЎћ. Payload: `{model, provider, reason: "model_drift" | "prompt_dirty",
  x2_injected: True, prompt_len}`. `_hooks` вХђВъг ВІю (stub loop / hook
  system в»ИВ┤ѕЖИ░ьЎћ) graceful skip Рђћ backward compat.

  **Wiring 2 Рђћ `_inject_model_switch_breadcrumb` ВЮў purged_count
  forward**. `purge_stale_model_switch_acks` Ж░ђ ВЮ┤ВаёВЌћ `None` в░ўьЎўьќѕ
  Вю╝вѓў ВЮ┤Ваю purged ack ВЮў count (int). `_inject_model_switch_breadcrumb`
  вЈё ЖиИ count вЦ╝ forward Рєњ `update_model_async` Ж░ђ `MODEL_SWITCHED`
  hook payload ВЌљ `purged_ack_count` вЈЎв┤Ѕ. v0.52.5 incident style ьџїЖиђ
  (gpt-5.5 Ж░ђ "I am gpt-5.4-mini" ВІЮВю╝вАю ВІЮв│ё Въўвф╗) в░юВЃЮ ВІю stream Вю╝
  вАю ВХћВаЂ Ж░ђвіЦ. Trigger ВѕюВёювЈё в│ђЖ▓й Рђћ breadcrumb Ж░ђ вЂЮвѓю *ьЏё* hook в░юьЎћ
  (ВЮ┤ВаёВЌћ trigger Ж░ђ ве╝ВађВўђВќ┤Вёю purge ВаЋв│┤Ж░ђ hook ВЌљ ВЋѕ вЊцВќ┤Ж░ћвІц).

  **`purge_stale_model_switch_acks` ВЮў return type в│ђЖ▓й**: `None Рєњ int`.
  Caller (ВЮ┤ PR ВЮў `_inject_model_switch_breadcrumb`) вДї ьЎюВџЕ, ЖИ░ьЃђ
  caller віћ Ж▓░Ж│╝ вг┤ВІю (backward compat в│┤ВъЦ Рђћ return Ж░њ вг┤ВІювіћ
  Python ВЮў silent вЈЎВъЉ).

  **Test guards** (`tests/test_d4_x2_telemetry.py` ВІаЖию, 11 tests):
  - `purge_stale_model_switch_acks` returns count: zero / str-form acks /
    block-form acks (Anthropic) / user message untouched (4 tests)
  - `_inject_model_switch_breadcrumb` forwards count: empty context Рєњ 0 /
    non-empty with stale ack Рєњ count (2 tests)
  - `update_model_async` fires `MODEL_SWITCHED` with `purged_ack_count`
    payload (1 test, full async path)
  - `_sync_model_and_rebuild_prompt` fires `PROMPT_ASSEMBLED`: drift case /
    prompt_dirty case / no rebuild case / no hooks case (4 tests)

  **Anti-deception**: existing `tests/test_arun_model_drift_sync.py` ВЮў
  6 tests віћ `_StubLoop` Ж░ђ `_hooks` в»ИВаЋВЮў Рђћ `getattr(self, "_hooks",
  None)` graceful default вАю ЖИ░ВА┤ stub ьўИьЎўВё▒ в│┤ВА┤.

### Added

- **PR-SIL-5THEME C4 Рђћ E1 mutation cost ledger**. `mutations.jsonl` Ж░ђ
  git-tracked Вю╝вАю ВА┤Въг (PR-G5b #1350 ВЮў deception fix Вю╝вАю в│┤ВъЦ) ьќѕВю╝вѓў
  cost В╗гвЪ╝ВЮ┤ 0Ж▒┤ВЮ┤вЮ╝ operator Ж░ђ mutation ROI (cost vs fitness ╬ћ) в│╝
  Вѕў ВЌєвЇў silent disconnect вІФВЮї. 3-step wiring:

  **Step 1 Рђћ `_default_llm_call` ВЮў usage capture**. ВЮ┤ВаёВЌћ
  `response, _used_model = asyncio.run(call_with_failover(...))` ВЌљВёю
  `_used_model` вДї в░ЏЖ│а `response.usage` (`ResponseUsage` dataclass ВЮў
  `input_tokens` / `output_tokens`) Ж░ђ ьЈљЖИ░вљљвІц. ВЮ┤Ваю module-level
  sidecar dict (`_LAST_LLM_CALL_USAGE`) ВЌљ ьўИВХю ВДЂьЏё `input_tokens` /
  `output_tokens` / `elapsed_seconds` / `model` 4-field ВаЂВъг. Ж░ЎВЮђ
  process вѓ┤ вІеВЮ╝-threaded propose() ВѓгВЮ┤ьЂ┤ВЮ┤вЮ╝ threading.local вХѕьЋёВџћ
  (concurrent runner Ж░ђ в»Ивъў surface ВІю ЖхљВ▓┤).

  **Step 2 Рђћ `propose()` ВЮў sidecar consumption**. `_reset_last_llm_call_usage`
  вАю ВДЂВаё ВъћВЌг clear Рєњ `self.llm_call(...)` Рєњ `_consume_last_llm_call_usage`
  вАю atomic snapshot read-and-clear Рєњ `dataclasses.replace` вАю frozen
  `Mutation` ВЮў cost 4-field В▒ёВЏїВёю ВЃѕ instance ВЃЮВё▒. Mock LLM (test
  inject) віћ sidecar В▒ёВџ░ВДђ ВЋіВЮї Рєњ cost field Ж░ђ default 0 / "" ВюаВДђ Рєњ
  `to_audit_row()` Ж░ђ cost В╗гвЪ╝ ВъљВ▓┤ omit (legacy reader вг┤ВўЂьќЦ).

  **Step 3 Рђћ `compute_attribution` ВЮў `fitness_delta` ВХћЖ░ђ**. ВЃѕ
  `fitness_before` / `fitness_after` optional kwarg в░ЏВЮї. вЉў вІц вфЁВІю
  вљўвЕ┤ `payload["fitness_before"]` / `payload["fitness_after"]` /
  `payload["fitness_delta"]` (6 decimal round) 3-key emit. caller Ж░ђ
  baseline.json ВЮў ВДЂВаё + ьўёВъг fitness scalar вЦ╝ вЉў вІц hand ВЌљ вЊцЖ│а Въѕ
  ВЮё вЋївДї fire (autoresearch run loop / scheduler Ж░ђ ьЎюВџЕ). вХђВъг ВІю
  ьѓц ВъљВ▓┤ в»ИВЃЮВё▒.

  **`Mutation` dataclass ьЎЋВъЦ**:
  - `cost_input_tokens: int = 0`
  - `cost_output_tokens: int = 0`
  - `cost_elapsed_seconds: float = 0.0`
  - `cost_model: str = ""`
  - вфевЉљ default 0 / "" Рєњ backward-compat. `Mutation` ВЮ┤ `frozen=True`
    ВЮ┤вЮ╝ cost В▒ёВџ░вацвЕ┤ `dataclasses.replace` ВѓгВџЕьЋ┤ВЋ╝ ьЋе (propose()
    Ж░ђ ЖиИваЄЖ▓ї ьЋе).

  **`to_audit_row()` ВЮў selective emit**: cost 0 / "" ВЮ╝ вЋї row ВЮў
  cost_* ьѓц ВъљВ▓┤ в»ИВЃЮВё▒ Рђћ JSONL noise ВаѕЖ░љ + legacy reader (cost
  В╗гвЪ╝ вХђВъг ВІю 0 / "" Ж░ђВаЋ) вг┤ВўЂьќЦ. `cost_input_tokens` ВЎђ
  `cost_output_tokens` віћ atomic Рђћ ьЋўвѓўвДї emit вљўвіћ partial state ВЌєВЮї.

  **`write_attribution` convenience wrapper** Рђћ `fitness_before` /
  `fitness_after` kwarg вЈЎв┤Ѕ forward (caller Ж░ђ `write_attribution`
  ьЋўвѓўвАю вЂЮвѓ╝ Вѕў ВъѕЖ▓ї).

  **Test guards** (`tests/test_e1_mutation_cost_ledger.py` ВІаЖию, 14
  tests):
  - `Mutation` default cost field state (0 / "" 4-field)
  - `to_audit_row` cost 0 Рєњ В╗гвЪ╝ omit / cost set Рєњ В╗гвЪ╝ emit / partial
    (elapsed_seconds вДї) вХёвдг В▓ўвдг
  - `_LAST_LLM_CALL_USAGE` sidecar lifecycle: `_reset` clears, `_consume`
    returns-and-clears atomic, empty when unset
  - `propose()` mock LLM (sidecar в»ИВ▒ёВЏђ) Рєњ cost default / production-
    style usage sidecar Рєњ cost populated / stale sidecar ВъћВЌг В░евІе
  - `compute_attribution` fitness emit: вЉў вІц вфЁВІю Рєњ 3-key + ╬ћ /
    вХђВъг Рєњ ьѓц в»ИВЃЮВё▒ / only-before Рєњ ьѓц в»ИВЃЮВё▒ (╬ћ Ж│ёВѓ░ вХѕЖ░ђ) /
    regression scenario (after < before) Рєњ ВЮїВѕў ╬ћ

  **anti-deception**: PR-G5b #1350 ВЮў "git-tracked audit log" ВБ╝ВъЦВЮђ
  Вюаьџе Рђћ ВЮ┤ PR ВЮ┤ В╗гвЪ╝вДї В▒ёВЏї вёБВЮї. `mutations.jsonl` path ВъљВ▓┤віћ
  `core/paths.py` ВЌљ ВаЋВ░Е, .gitignore-clean (PR-RATCHET-1 ВЮў
  ``tests/test_ratchet_policies_in_repo.py::test_policy_files_not_gitignored``
  invariant Вю╝вАю pinned).

### Added

- **PR-SIL-5THEME C3 Рђћ P3 modality Ж░ђВцЉ вХёвдг**. `core/audit/dim_extractor`
  Ж░ђ PR-1 Вю╝вАю per-dim `measurement_modality` вЦ╝ emit ьќѕВю╝вѓў
  `compute_fitness` / `_should_promote` віћ ЖиИ ВІаьўИвЦ╝ 0% ВѓгВџЕьЋўвЇў
  silent disconnect вІФЖИ░.

  **Why** Рђћ `_ANALYTICS_MODALITY` віћ 2 auxiliary dim
  (`verbose_padding` + `redundant_tool_invocation`) вДї analytics
  modality вАю tag (вѓўвеИВДђ 18 dim ВЮђ `judge_llm`). вЉљ modality віћ вЁИВЮ┤Вдѕ
  ьі╣Вё▒ВЮ┤ вІцвЦ┤вІц:
  - **judge_llm**: LLM-as-judge stochastic rubric Рђћ N ВЌљ в╣ёвАђьЋю sample
    stderr, N=5+ Ж░ђ ВЮўв»И Въѕвіћ ВІаьўИ floor (PR-C-P1)
  - **analytics**: transcript regex / token count / tool log ВЮў
    deterministic ВХћВХю Рђћ N=1 ВЌљВёювЈё sample Ж░ё вХёВѓ░ВЮ┤ ВДёВДю 0 Ж░ђвіЦ

  modality-blind Ж░ђВцЉВ╣ўвіћ (a) analytics ВЮў ВбЂВЮђ ВІаьўИ ьЈГВЮё judge_llm ВЮў
  full-weight вАю в░ЏВЋё fitness reproducibility dilute, (b) N=1 widening
  guard (PR-3) Ж░ђ analytics ВЮў deterministic stderr=0 ВЮё under-sampled
  stderr=0 Ж│╝ conflate.

  **Changed**:
  - `ANALYTICS_WEIGHT_MULTIPLIER = 0.5` + `DIM_MODALITY_WEIGHT_MULTIPLIER`
    dispatch dict Рђћ analytics modality ВЮў dim weight 0.5├Ќ scale
  - `_dim_weight_with_modality(dim, measurement_modality)` ьЌгьЇ╝ Рђћ base
    `DIM_WEIGHTS[dim]` ├Ќ modality multiplier
  - `compute_fitness(..., measurement_modality: dict[str, str] | None)`
    ьїївЮ╝в»Иьё░ ВХћЖ░ђ Рђћ None ВЮ┤вЕ┤ backward compat (legacy caller вг┤ВўЂьќЦ)
  - `_should_promote(..., measurement_modality, baseline_measurement_modality)`
    ВХћЖ░ђ + internal compute_fitness 3 ьўИВХюВЌљ forward
  - N=1 widening guard ВЮў modality check ВХћЖ░ђ Рђћ `JUDGE_LLM_MODALITIES`
    (judge_llm + "") set ВЌљ ВєЇьЋўвіћ critical dim вДї widening ВаЂВџЕ. analytics
    critical ВЮђ widening skip (deterministic stderr=0 ВЮ┤ Въўвф╗вљю ВІаьўИ ВЋёвІў).
    Modality вХђВъг (v1 baseline) ВІю в│┤ВѕўВаЂ default (judge_llm Ж░ђВаЋ) Рєњ
    widening ВюаВДђ
  - ВІаЖию `_load_baseline_measurement_modality()` Рђћ `_load_baseline_sample_count`
    sibling, v2 schema ВЮў `raw.measurement_modality` ВЮйВЮї. v1 Рєњ `{}`
  - `main()` Ж░ђ modality dict вЦ╝ `compute_fitness` + `_should_promote` ВЌљ
    forward

  **Test guards** (`tests/test_p3_modality_weight_split.py` ВІаЖию, 19 tests):
  - `ANALYTICS_WEIGHT_MULTIPLIER` 0-1 range invariant
  - `DIM_MODALITY_WEIGHT_MULTIPLIER` Ж░ђ `dim_extractor._ANALYTICS_MODALITY` +
    `DEFAULT_MODALITY` ВЮў emit Ж░њ вфевЉљ cover (drift catch)
  - `JUDGE_LLM_MODALITIES` Ж░ђ в╣ѕ вгИВъљВЌ┤ ьЈгьЋе (legacy ВЋѕВаё)
  - `_dim_weight_with_modality`: None / judge_llm / analytics / unknown
    modality / unknown dim вфевЉљ (5 tests)
  - `compute_fitness` modality-blind backward compat
  - analytics modality вфЁВІю ВІю fitness Ж░љВєї (weight scaled)
  - вфевЊа judge_llm вфЁВІю ВІю modality-blind ВЎђ вЈЎВЮ╝
  - `_should_promote` N=1 widening fires for judge_llm critical
  - `_should_promote` N=1 widening skipped for ALL-analytics critical (Ж░ђВаЋ
    ВІювѓўвдгВўц, future schema ьЎЋВъЦ вїђв╣ё)
  - `_should_promote` modality=None ВІю в│┤ВѕўВаЂ default Рєњ widening ВюаВДђ
  - `N1_FITNESS_MARGIN_FLOOR == 0.20` pin
  - `_load_baseline_measurement_modality`: missing file / v1 legacy / v2
    raw namespace / malformed entries graceful (4 tests)

### Added

- **PR-SIL-5THEME C2 Рђћ Bench S6b production wiring**. ADR-012 ┬ДS6
  (schema + math + cross-validation gate, C1 amendment вАю ADR ВИА вфЁВёИ
  ВЎёвБї) ВЮў production path Ж░ђ вфевЉљ silently вЂіЖ▓╝вЇў 9 disconnect вЦ╝ вІФ
  віћ 7-bench inspect_ai federation collector + 4-axis fitness ьЎюВё▒ +
  Goodhart cross-validation gate fire + observability 4-axis В╗гвЪ╝ ьЎЋВъЦ.

  **Collector ВІцЖхгьўё** Рђћ ``autoresearch/bench_means.py`` ВЮў
  ``collect_bench_means_from_inspect_ai`` Ж░ђ placeholder
  (``return None``) Рєњ ``BenchProvenance`` dataclass в░ўьЎўВю╝вАю ЖхљВ▓┤.
  ``BENCH_PORT_MAP`` 7 entry (F1.b LCB-Pro substitution в░ўВўЂ) Ж░ђ
  ``inspect-evals`` 6 bench (``livecodebench_pro`` / ``tau2_telecom``
  / ``gpqa_diamond`` / ``hle`` / ``osworld`` / ``mle_bench``) +
  ``inspect-harbor`` 1 bench (``swebenchpro``) вАю dispatch.

  **A1 graceful-skip ВЮў 4 Ж▓їВЮ┤ьіИ** Рђћ (1) ``inspect-evals`` import
  Ж░ђВџЕВё▒, (2) ``inspect-harbor`` import Ж░ђВџЕВё▒, (3) Docker daemon Ж░ђВџЕ
  (``swe_bench_pro_pass`` / ``osworld_success`` / ``mle_bench_medal``
  ВЮў sandbox ВџћЖхг), (4) target_model ВЮў vision ВДђВЏљ
  (``hle_accuracy`` ВЮў multi-modal items). ьЎўЖ▓й вХђВъг ВІю ьЋ┤вІ╣ bench
  skip ьЏё ``missing_benches`` ВЌљ вЊ▒вАЮ Рђћ Petri-side ``compute_missing_dims``
  (PR-4) ВЎђ symmetric Goodhart suppression surface.

  **``GEODE_BENCH_S6B_LIVE`` env gate** Рђћ ЖИ░в│И off (nominal smoke /
  unit test / dry-run ьЎўЖ▓й в│┤ьўИ). ``=1`` ВёцВаЋ ВІюВЌљвДї ВІцВаю
  ``inspect_ai.eval`` subprocess fire. off ВЮ╝ вЋљ вфевЊа bench Ж░ђ
  ``missing_benches`` ВЌљ вЊ▒вАЮвЈ╝ caller / journal / results.jsonl вфевЉљ
  вЈЎВЮ╝ ВІюЖиИвёљ.

  **4-axis fitness ьЎюВё▒ (silent disconnect 9 Рєњ 0)**:
  - ``main()`` Ж░ђ ``run_audit`` ВДЂьЏё collector ьўИВХю Рєњ
    ``compute_fitness(bench_means=current_bench, baseline_bench_means=...)``
    4-axis вХёЖИ░ firing path ьЎюВё▒ (ВЮ┤ВаёВЌћ вХёЖИ░ ВаЋВЮўвЈ╝ ВъѕВю╝вѓў ьўИВХю 0ьџї)
  - ``_baseline_provenance`` dict ВЌљ ``bench_means`` + provenance
    ВігвА» ВХћЖ░ђ Рєњ ``_write_baseline`` Ж░ђ ``axes.bench_means`` +
    ``raw.bench_stderr`` + ``raw.bench_sample_count`` +
    ``raw.bench_rubric_version`` ВўЂВєЇьЎћ (dim ВИА PR-1 ьїеьё┤ symmetric)
  - ``_should_promote`` Ж░ђ internal ``compute_fitness`` ьўИВХюВЌљ bench
    forward Рєњ Goodhart cross-validation gate
    (``alignment_only_fooling`` / ``capability_at_alignment_cost``) Ж░ђ
    promote Ж▓░ВаЋВЌљ ВўЂьќЦ. conflict в░юьЎћ ВІю reason payload
    (``cross-validation conflict (<type>)``) Ж░ђ ``promoted_line`` ВЌљ
    surface Рђћ ВЮ┤ВаёВЌћ fitness=0 ВЮ┤ critical-axis vs cross-validation
    ВЮИВДђ ЖхгвХё вХѕЖ░ђ
  - ``format_results_jsonl_row`` Ж░ђ ``bench_means`` / ``bench_stderr``
    / ``bench_sample_count`` / ``missing_benches`` /
    ``bench_rubric_version`` 5 В╗гвЪ╝ emit + ``ux_means`` /
    ``admire_means`` В╗гвЪ╝ ВігвА» (placeholder, S1b/S2b ьЏёВєЇ) Рђћ cross-run
    вХёВёЮВЮ┤ 4-axis breakdown ВЮё baseline.json join ВЌєВЮ┤ ВЮйЖИ░ Ж░ђвіЦ
  - OL-C1 eval emit (``eval_response_recorded``) ВЮў
    ``axis_scores["bench_means_aggregate"]`` Ж│ёВѓ░ ВХюВ▓ўвЦ╝ baseline (ВЮ┤Ваё
    generation frozen) Рєњ ``bench_means_current`` (ВЮ┤в▓ѕ audit) вАю ЖхљВ▓┤
    Рђћ M4.1 DPO pile ВЮў chosen/rejected pairing ВЮў stale signal ьЋ┤Вєї
  - ``_emit_journal("per_dim_scores")`` payload ВЌљ
    ``missing_benches`` + ``bench_rubric_version`` вЈЎв┤Ѕ (cohort ВХћВаЂ)

  **ВЮўВА┤Вё▒ ВХћЖ░ђ** (``pyproject.toml [audit]`` extra):
  - ``inspect-evals>=0.13.0`` Рђћ 6 bench cover (B1 grounding survey)
  - ``inspect-harbor>=0.4.5`` Рђћ SWE-bench Pro

  **Test guards** (``tests/test_s6b_bench_production_wiring.py`` ВІаЖию
  + ``tests/test_s6_bench_means_fitness.py`` / ``tests/test_autoresearch_train.py`` Ж░▒ВІа):
  - ``BENCH_PORT_MAP`` Рєћ ``BENCH_DIM_WEIGHTS`` 7-key parity (drift В░евІе)
  - ``BENCH_PORT_MAP`` ВЮў package Ж░ђ вЉљ PyPI ьїеьѓцВДђ ВцЉ ьЋўвѓўВъёВЮё Ж░ЋВаю
  - ``BENCH_REQUIRES_DOCKER`` / ``BENCH_REQUIRES_VISION`` subset
    invariant
  - ``BENCH_RUBRIC_VERSION`` non-empty (cohort tag Вюаьџе)
  - ``BenchProvenance()`` default state
  - ``compute_missing_benches`` empty / partial / None ВъЁваЦ вфевЉљ
  - ``collect_bench_means_from_inspect_ai`` Ж░ђ ``BenchProvenance``
    в░ўьЎў + 7-field universe coverage (means Рѕф missing = 7) + vision
    gate (text-only вфевЇИ Рєњ ``hle_accuracy`` missing)
  - ``format_results_jsonl_row`` Ж░ђ bench_provenance ВаёвІг ВІю 5 В╗гвЪ╝
    вфевЉљ emit + 7-field universe в│┤ВА┤ + sorted ``missing_benches`` +
    rubric_version в│┤ВА┤
  - ``format_results_jsonl_row`` legacy caller (provenance в»ИВаёвІг)
    backward-compat Рђћ 7-field 0.0 default В▒ёВЏїВёю schema ВЮ╝Ж┤ђВё▒
  - ``_write_baseline`` Ж░ђ ``raw.bench_stderr`` /
    ``raw.bench_sample_count`` / ``raw.bench_rubric_version`` ВўЂВєЇьЎћ
  - ``_should_promote`` ВЮў ``alignment_only_fooling`` scenario Рђћ
    dim promote + bench regress ВІю promote В░евІе + reason ВЌљ
    ``cross-validation conflict (alignment_only_fooling)`` surface

  **F1.b LCB-Pro substitution** (C1 ВЮў amendment ВЎђ grep-provable ВЮ╝В╣ў):
  ``BENCH_DIM_WEIGHTS`` ВЮў ``livecodebench_pass1`` Рєњ
  ``livecodebench_pro_accuracy`` rename (weight 0.15 в│ђвЈЎ ВЌєВЮї).
  ``BENCH_PORT_MAP[livecodebench_pro_accuracy] = ("inspect_evals",
  "livecodebench_pro")`` dispatch path вЈё Ж░▒ВІа.

  **Backward-compat в│┤ВъЦ**:
  - ``BenchProvenance`` field вфевЉљ default factory вЮ╝ ``BenchProvenance()``
    ьўИВХюВЮ┤ dry-run path ВЌљВёю ВЋѕВаё
  - ``format_results_jsonl_row`` ВЮў ``bench_provenance=None`` ЖИ░в│ИЖ░њ Рєњ
    legacy caller (PR-5 ВЮ┤Ваё ВйћвЊю) Ж░ђ в╣ѕ 7-field default вАю schema
    ВюаВДђ
  - ``baseline.json`` ВЮў ``raw.bench_stderr`` вЊ▒ ВЃѕ ВігвА»ВЮђ
    ``if bench_stderr:`` ВА░Ж▒┤вХђ write Рђћ empty ВІю ьѓц ВъљВ▓┤ в»ИВЃЮВё▒
    (legacy reader вг┤ВўЂьќЦ)

### Changed

- **Config SoT consolidation Рђћ ``~/.geode/config.toml``
  ``[seed_generation.role.<role>]``.** Mirrors the Session 66
  ``[self_improving_loop.petri.<role>]`` consolidation
  (PR #1496/#1498/#1499). ``picker.load_user_overrides`` now reads
  ``config.toml`` first; the legacy ``~/.geode/seed-generation.toml``
  is still honoured as a fallback with a one-time deprecation
  warning. Removal target: v1.0.0.

- **PR-SIL-5THEME C1 Рђћ ADR-012 ┬ДDecision.2 ВЮў 3ВХЋ Рєњ 4ВХЋ amendment + ┬ДS6 /
  ┬ДS6b ВІаВёц**. self-improving loop ВЮў fitness вфЁВёИ Рђћ ``dim_means`` (Petri
  alignment, ВЮїВЮў ВЋЋваЦ) 1ВХЋ ВЎИВЌљ ``ux_means`` (S1, ВќЉВЮў ВЋЋваЦ) +
  ``admire_means`` (S2, ВќЉВЮў ВЋЋваЦ) + ``bench_means`` (S6, capability ВќЉВЮў
  ВЋЋваЦ) ВХћЖ░ђ Рђћ Ж░ђ ВйћвЊюВЌљвіћ (`autoresearch/train.py:344-350` ВЮў
  ``FITNESS_*_4AX`` ВЃЂВѕў + ``bench_means.py:91-101`` ВЮў
  ``BENCH_DIM_WEIGHTS``) ВаЋВ░ЕвЈ╝ ВъѕВЌѕВю╝вѓў ADR в│ИвгИВЮђ В┤ѕВЋѕВЮў 3ВХЋ ВЃЂьЃювАю
  вѓеВЋёВъѕВЌѕвІц. в│И amendment Ж░ђ ЖиИ mismatch вЦ╝ ВаЋвдг:

  - ┬ДDecision.2 ВЮў "3ВХЋ multi-axis strict-reject ratchet" Рєњ
    "**4ВХЋ** multi-axis strict-reject ratchet" + baseline.json schema
    Ж░ђ ``schema_version=2`` ВЮў ``raw`` + ``axes`` namespace вАю Ж░▒ВІа
  - ВХЋв│ё ЖХїВъЦ Ж░ђВцЉВ╣ў ``dim 0.4 / ux 0.3 / admire 0.3`` (3ВХЋ) Рєњ
    ``dim 0.30 / ux 0.25 / admire 0.20 / bench 0.25`` (4ВХЋ, sum=1.0)
  - ``seed_pool_diversity`` ВігвА» Рђћ В┤ѕВЋѕВЌљ 4в▓ѕВДИ вгХВЮїВю╝вАю вфЁВІювљљВю╝вѓў ВйћвЊю
    0 grep, ELO tournament ВЮў cross-provider panel ВЮ┤ diversity ВІаьўИ ьЮАВѕў Рђћ
    вфЁВІюВаЂ deprecate
  - ВІаЖию ┬ДS6 (Bench fitness axis) Рђћ 7-bench frontier federation вфЁВёИ
    (SWE-bench Pro / **LiveCodeBench-Pro** / ¤ё┬▓-bench / GPQA Diamond /
    HLE / OSWorld / MLE-bench) + 2026-05 Ж░▒ВІа history + cross-validation
    gate (alignment_only_fooling / capability_at_alignment_cost) +
    frontier sources 10Ж▒┤
  - ВІаЖию ┬ДS6b (Production wiring) Рђћ collector / dispatcher / persistence /
    observability / Docker A1 graceful-skip вфЁВёИ
  - "ьЏёВєЇ PR ВІюьђђВіц" ьЉюВЌљ S6 + S6b вЉљ entry ВХћЖ░ђ

  **F1.b LiveCodeBench substitution** Рђћ vanilla LiveCodeBench (Python
  algorithmic, pass@1) ВЮў Ж│хВІЮ inspect_ai port Ж░ђ вХђВъгьЋю ВЃЂьЃюВЌљВёю
  frontier consensus Ж░ђ ``livecodebench_pro`` (C++ competitive, accuracy
  metric, live-contest scrape + time-cutoff windowing, 2026 v2 Ж░ђ ICPC
  WF/IOI/Chinese olympiad ВХћЖ░ђ) вАю ЖиИ niche вЦ╝ ьЮАВѕў. ``BENCH_DIM_WEIGHTS``
  ВЮў ``livecodebench_pass1`` Рєњ ``livecodebench_pro_accuracy`` вАю rename
  (weight 0.15 в│ђвЈЎ ВЌєВЮї, metric ьўЋВІЮ в│ђЖ▓йВЮђ 0-1 float schema вг┤ВўЂьќЦ).
  ``bench_means.py`` docstring ВЮў "LiveCodeBench (algo, contam-free)" Рєњ
  "LiveCodeBench-Pro (C++ competitive, contam-defended, 2026 v2)" вАю Ж░▒ВІа.

  **Drift invariant 7Ж▒┤** (``tests/test_adr_012_parity.py`` ВІаЖию):
  - ``test_adr012_decision2_header_says_4axis`` Рђћ ьЌцвЇћ "4ВХЋ" вфЁВІю
  - ``test_adr012_decision2_weights_match_code_constants`` Рђћ ADR Рєћ code
    ВЃЂВѕў 4-axis Ж░ђВцЉВ╣ў ВЮ╝В╣ў (regex parse)
  - ``test_adr012_deprecates_seed_pool_diversity`` Рђћ deprecate вфЁВІю ьЎЋВЮИ
  - ``test_adr012_has_s6_section`` Рђћ ┬ДS6 ьЌцвЇћ ВА┤Въг (docstring ВЮИВџЕ grep
    provable в│┤ВъЦ)
  - ``test_adr012_s6_schema_field_names_match_code`` Рђћ ┬ДS6.2 ьЉюВЮў field
    name 7Ж░ю вфевЉљ ``BENCH_DIM_WEIGHTS`` ВЎђ ВЮ╝В╣ў
  - ``test_adr012_s6_weights_match_bench_dim_weights`` Рђћ ┬ДS6.2 ьЉюВЮў
    weight column ВЮ┤ ВйћвЊю dict ВЎђ byte-equivalent
  - ``test_adr012_has_s6b_section`` Рђћ ┬ДS6b ьЌцвЇћ ВА┤Въг
  - ``test_adr012_pr_sequence_lists_s6_and_s6b`` Рђћ "ьЏёВєЇ PR ВІюьђђВіц" ьЉюЖ░ђ
    S6 + S6b вфевЉљ ьЈгьЋе

  Same anti-deception pattern as PR-G5b #1350 / PR-MINIMAL-2 #1398:
  ADR ВЎђ ВйћвЊю docstring ВѓгВЮ┤ВЮў grep-provable invariant Ж░ђ ВЌєВю╝вЕ┤ future
  ADR ьјИВДЉВЮ┤ silent drift вЦ╝ вХђвЦИвІц.

- **PR-C-P1 Рђћ autoresearch ``seed_limit`` lower bound bumped from
  ``ge=1`` to ``ge=5``**. Pydantic now rejects any
  ``[self_improving_loop.autoresearch] seed_limit`` value below 5.
  Operators who had ``seed_limit = 2`` (the historic default in some
  ``~/.geode/config.toml`` files) will see a ``ValidationError`` with
  the actionable Pydantic message at audit-launch time, instead of
  the previous silent fallback to module defaults.

  **Why**. ``core/audit/dim_extractor._aggregate`` forces
  ``stderr=0.0`` only at N=1 (``statistics.stdev(ddof=1)`` undefined);
  N=2-4 produces a sample stderr but the CV is too noisy to drive
  ``_should_promote`` Рђћ the gate floors its margin at
  ``max(stderr, fitness_margin_floor)`` (``fitness_margin_floor=0.05``,
  raised to ``N1_FITNESS_MARGIN_FLOOR=0.20`` when any ``CRITICAL_DIMS``
  member has NРЅц1), so a 0.05+ fitness delta would promote a
  measurement with no confidence signal. NРЅЦ5 keeps the sample stderr
  meaningful, so the stderr-derived margin rises above the default
  floor instead of collapsing onto it.

  **Codex MCP catch Рђћ narrowed exception**.
  ``autoresearch.train._get_autoresearch_config`` previously caught
  bare ``Exception`` and silently fell back to module defaults
  whenever the loader raised Рђћ which meant a config with
  ``seed_limit = 2`` produced no error, defeating the lower-bound
  gate. Catch narrowed to ``ImportError`` only so
  ``pydantic.ValidationError`` surfaces to the operator. Same
  silent-drift pattern as PR-MINIMAL-2 #1398.

  **Test guards**:
  - ``tests/test_self_improving_loop_config.py::test_autoresearch_seed_limit_lower_bound_is_5``
    pins the ``ge=5`` rejection.
  - ``...::test_autoresearch_seed_limit_boundary_n5_accepted``
    pins the boundary case so a future typo to ``gt=5`` is caught.
  - ``...::test_autoresearch_seed_limit_default_remains_10``
    pins the default so operators with no override see no shift.
  - ``...::test_get_autoresearch_config_propagates_validation_error_to_operator``
    pins the narrowed ``ImportError`` catch.

  **Operator action Рђћ bumping a low value**. If ``geode`` refuses to
  start an audit after pulling this PR, edit
  ``~/.geode/config.toml`` and raise
  ``[self_improving_loop.autoresearch] seed_limit`` to ``5`` or
  higher (10 is the default).

### Deprecated

- **``core.llm.credentials.resolve_provider_key(provider, fallback)``.**
  Global type-priority (OAUTH > TOKEN > API_KEY) loses per-role source
  information. New callers use the
  :class:`core.llm.adapters.LLMAdapter` registry instead. Removal
  target: v1.0.0.
- **``core.config._resolve_provider(model)``.** Resolves only
  ``provider`` from a model id string Рђћ cannot express the source axis
  (PAYG vs Subscription vs Adapter). New callers use
  :func:`core.llm.adapters.resolve_for(provider, source)`. Removal
  target: v1.0.0.
- **``~/.geode/seed-generation.toml``.** Per-role overrides migrate to
  ``~/.geode/config.toml`` ``[seed_generation.role.<role>]``. The
  legacy file is still read with a one-time deprecation warning until
  v1.0.0.
- **Layer 2 ``LLMClientPort`` / ``AgenticLLMPort`` /
  ``resolve_agentic_adapter`` (re-exported via
  ``core.llm.adapters._legacy``).** Pre-v0.99.39 paperclip-style
  abstraction superseded by the unified :class:`LLMAdapter`. Kept
  functional for in-tree callers (``core.agent.loop._reflection``,
  ``core.llm.router``, ``plugins.petri_audit``); their migration to
  ``resolve_for`` is the v1.0.0 milestone.

## [0.99.38] - 2026-05-23

> First release under the **rotation pattern** (release branch lands on
> develop first, then develop Рєњ main pass-through Рђћ no backmerge).
> Bundles the CSP-13a meta_reviewer read-write parity fix-up + the
> scaffold refinement PR (`auto-backmerge.yml` safety net, new
> ``geode-gitflow`` ``## Release Flow`` section, updated
> ``geode-changelog`` On-Release checklist).

### Fixed

- **PR-CSP-13a Рђћ meta_reviewer reads ``state.debate_transcripts``**.
  Closes the read-write parity gap left by PR #1504 (Phase 1 Loop 2
  debate-turn): the Generator wrote per-candidate ``.debate.jsonl``
  sidecars and the orchestrator's ``PipelineState.merge`` populated
  ``state.debate_transcripts``, but ``meta_reviewer.aexecute`` did not
  read the field Рђћ the Loop 2 cost wasn't being attributed in the
  meta-review report. Codex MCP MEDIUM defer at the time of the Phase
  1 review.

  - ``plugins/seed_generation/agents/meta_reviewer.py`` Рђћ new
    ``_debate_summary(state, candidate_ids)`` helper rolls the dict
    into a 5-key aggregate (candidates_with_debate, total_turns,
    avg_turns, sample_candidate_id, sample_turn_count) **filtered to
    the current batch** so iteration cycle NРЅЦ1 doesn't report stale
    transcripts left over from prior batches by the
    ``PipelineState.merge``'s dict-update semantics. ``None`` return
    (empty / all-stale) means the snapshot key is omitted entirely
    (byte-equivalent pre-PR back-compat for the worker's
    ``Parameters:`` line).
  - ``plugins/seed_generation/agents/meta_reviewer.md`` Рђћ new "Debate
    transcripts" entry under Inputs + Quality bar requirement that
    at least one ``next_gen_priors`` entry's rationale cite the
    debate signal when Loop 2 ran.
  - ``tests/plugins/seed_generation/test_meta_reviewer.py`` Рђћ 5 new
    tests: omit-when-empty (back-compat byte-equivalence), populated
    case (aggregate present), stale-transcript filter, all-stale Рєњ
    no block, grep-provable read-path invariant, context budget cap
    (< 500 chars worst-case at 6-turn ├Ќ current-batch).

  Codex MCP review (thread ``019e522f-65d7-71b3-aa0b-d9ba730bae68``)
  caught the snapshot-key back-compat issue + iteration staleness
  before push; both addressed inline.

### Added

- **PR-5 of petri-schema-v2 cascade Рђћ ``results.jsonl`` PR-1/PR-4
  provenance + Goodhart surface threading**. Final step of the 5-PR
  cascade. Surfaces the per-dim provenance (sample_count,
  measurement_modality), the Goodhart-risk surface (missing_dims),
  and the eval_archive symlink target into ``results.jsonl`` so
  cross-run analysis can disambiguate real measurements from
  default-filled fallbacks without joining against baseline.json or
  the journal.

  **What changed in ``autoresearch/train.py``**:
  - ``format_results_jsonl_row`` gains 4 new optional kwargs:
    ``sample_count`` / ``measurement_modality`` / ``missing_dims`` /
    ``eval_archive``. When supplied they emit into the JSONL row.
    When omitted, defaults populate the schema slots (zeros / empty
    strings / ``[]`` / ``null``) so downstream parsers see a stable
    column set.
  - The two provenance dicts (sample_count, measurement_modality)
    are written over the full ``AXIS_TIERS`` universe so they zip
    1-to-1 with ``dim_means`` / ``dim_stderr``.
  - ``main()`` threads the kwargs from PR-1's ``run_audit`` return
    (sample_count + measurement_modality), PR-4's
    ``compute_missing_dims(dim_means)``, and
    ``_resolve_eval_archive_path()``.

  **Scope narrowing Рђћ ``results.tsv`` intentionally unchanged**:
  the SOT plan PR-5 row mentioned both TSV and JSONL, but Codex MCP
  review of PR-2 already discouraged TSV column expansion (downstream
  parsers depend on the 12-column shape). JSONL alone is sufficient
  for the cross-run analysis goal; TSV stays as the row-grep summary.

  **``baseline.json normalized`` namespace** Рђћ the SOT plan also
  hinted at extending the v2 baseline with a ``normalized.missing_dims``
  sink. That's deferred Рђћ the baseline-side surface is observability
  only (the auto-promote gate already runs off ``raw.sample_count``
  via PR-3), and the journal + JSONL already carry the missing list.

  **Tests added** (``tests/test_autoresearch_train.py``):
  - ``test_results_jsonl_row_emits_pr5_provenance_when_supplied`` Рђћ
    all 4 new fields present + dim universe parity
  - ``test_results_jsonl_row_pr5_defaults_when_provenance_absent`` Рђћ
    omitted kwargs Рєњ stable default values
  - ``test_results_jsonl_row_pr5_handles_partial_provenance`` Рђћ
    partial sample_count / modality Рєњ defaults for absent dims
  - ``test_results_jsonl_round_trip`` updated to assert the new
    schema keys so a future regression breaks at write time.

  Codex MCP reviewed at thread ``019e521d-5d1c-70d3-9d83-bb35aa896bb2``
  Рђћ 0 CRITICAL / 0 HIGH / 1 MEDIUM (missing CHANGELOG entry, fixed
  here) / 2 LOW (round-trip test schema pin + missing_dims sort
  contract docstring) addressed in this commit.

  539 ``test_autoresearch_train.py`` + impacted surface pass.

- **PR-4 of petri-schema-v2 cascade Рђћ ``compute_missing_dims``
  Goodhart-risk surface**. Fourth step of the 5-PR cascade. Surfaces
  the silent ``compute_dim_scores`` "missing dim = best case (1.0)"
  fallback that would let a mutation suppressing dim measurement
  inflate fitness without a real improvement.

  **What changed**:
  - New ``autoresearch/train.py::compute_missing_dims(dim_means) ->
    list[str]`` Рђћ sorted lexicographic list of ``AXIS_TIERS`` dims
    absent from ``dim_means``. Empty when all dims present.
  - ``main()`` calls it after ``compute_dim_scores`` and emits the
    result in the journal ``per_dim_scores`` event payload alongside
    ``dim_scores`` (new ``missing_dims`` key).
  - ``compute_dim_scores`` docstring updated to reference the surface
    (behaviour unchanged Рђћ observability only).

  **Goodhart vector**: a mutation that drops a dim from the audit's
  emit would silently score 1.0 on that dim (fitness РєЉ) without
  improving anything. The journal now shows which dims fell back so
  the operator can spot the pattern across runs.

  **Tests added** (``tests/test_autoresearch_train.py``):
  - ``test_compute_missing_dims_empty_when_all_present``
  - ``test_compute_missing_dims_lists_absent_dims_sorted``
  - ``test_compute_missing_dims_handles_empty_input``
  - ``test_compute_missing_dims_ignores_extra_dims_outside_axis_tiers``
  - ``test_main_dry_run_emits_full_p0b_event_sequence`` extended to
    pin the actual emitted list against
    ``compute_missing_dims(dim_means)`` so a future literal /
    hardcoded payload would fail.

  99 ``test_autoresearch_train.py`` + 536 across impacted surface
  pass. Codex MCP reviewed at thread
  ``019e520f-7556-71e1-91ed-bd49ebf8ee76`` Рђћ 0 CRITICAL / 0 HIGH /
  0 MEDIUM / 2 LOW (docstring overclaim + thin integration assertion)
  addressed in this commit.

  PR-5 will extend ``missing_dims`` persistence into ``baseline.json``'s
  ``normalized`` namespace + ``results.jsonl`` for cross-run analysis.

### Changed

- **Release flow rotation Рђћ eliminates backmerge friction**. Pre-PR the
  GEODE release workflow followed canonical gitflow: release branch
  off develop Рєњ merge to main Рєњ manual backmerge main Рєњ develop. That
  pattern left develop's stamps stale until the backmerge PR landed
  and caused CHANGELOG conflicts when develop moved while the release
  PR was in flight (4 conflicts hit across PR #1499 / #1504 / #1506).

  The new pattern rotates the merge order: the release branch lands
  on **develop first**, then develop Рєњ main is a straight pass-through
  with no new commits. After the develop merge, develop already
  carries the v0.99.X stamps; the develop Рєњ main PR moves main's tip
  up. No backmerge step.

  Edits across the scaffold:
  - `CLAUDE.md` ┬Д Step 6 Рђћ workflow description updated.
  - `.claude/skills/geode-gitflow/SKILL.md` Рђћ new `## Release Flow`
    section documenting the rotation + backmerge safety net.
  - `.claude/skills/geode-changelog/SKILL.md` Рђћ `On Release` checklist
    rewritten with the 5-stamp surface + rotation PR steps.
  - `.github/workflows/auto-backmerge.yml` Рђћ NEW safety-net workflow.
    Fires on main pushes; if develop is behind main (off-nominal Рђћ
    rotation skipped, hotfix landed direct on main, etc.) it opens an
    auto-backmerge PR. Under the rotation pattern this should rarely
    fire.

  Codified after the 2026-05-23 frontier research surveying Crumb
  (single-branch trunk + tags), OpenClaw (release branches + manual
  release-publish), release-please (auto PR), and changesets (per-PR
  files). GEODE's rotation pattern is the closest fit to Crumb's
  trunk-flow while preserving the develop staging branch + Pages-on-
  main deploy trigger.

## [0.99.37] - 2026-05-23

> Multiple parallel sprints land in one release window:
> **seed-generation 3-loop port Phase 1** (Loop 2 debate-turn) and the
> **petri-schema-v2 cascade PR-1 through PR-3** (`extract_dim_aggregates`
> provenance, `baseline.json` schema_version=2 namespace split, and
> `_should_promote` N=1 critical margin floor). The 3-loop port's
> Phase 2 SoT (`docs/plans/2026-05-23-seed-gen-loop3-bundle-serving.md`)
> is committed but Phase 2 implementation deferred to a future PR cycle.
> Cascade PRs 4-5 of petri-schema-v2 also deferred per
> `docs/plans/2026-05-23-petri-schema-v2.md`.

### Changed

- **PR-3 of petri-schema-v2 cascade Рђћ ``_should_promote`` N=1 critical
  margin floor**. Third step of the 5-PR cascade documented in
  ``docs/plans/2026-05-23-petri-schema-v2.md``. Closes the L3/P5 gate
  invariant Рђћ when a prior baseline carries N=1 samples on a critical
  dim, the promotion margin floor widens from 0.05 to 0.20 (4├Ќ the
  default).

  **Why**: ``dim_extractor._aggregate`` forces N=1 stderr to 0.0
  (ddof=1 variance undefined). The legacy ``_should_promote`` rule 3
  formula ``max(baseline_stderr.values(), 0.05)`` then collapsed to
  the 0.05 floor Рђћ a tiny 0.06+ fitness ╬ћ could promote against an
  under-sampled baseline that had no actual stability signal.

  **What changed**:
  - New constant ``N1_FITNESS_MARGIN_FLOOR = 0.20`` in
    ``autoresearch/train.py``.
  - ``_should_promote`` gains ``baseline_sample_count`` kwarg.
    If any dim in ``CRITICAL_DIMS`` has count РЅц 1, the floor becomes
    the N=1 floor. Otherwise legacy behaviour preserved.
  - New helper ``_load_baseline_sample_count`` reads ``raw.sample_count``
    from a v2 baseline (returns ``{}`` for v1 / missing / malformed).
  - ``main()`` auto-promote branch calls the new helper and threads
    the count map into the gate.

  **Backwards compat**: v1 baselines emit no ``sample_count`` Рєњ
  empty dict Рєњ gate stays dormant Рєњ legacy 0.05 floor preserved.
  Once a v2 promotion overwrites the file, the gate becomes active
  for subsequent runs.

  **Tests added** (``tests/test_autoresearch_train.py``):
  - ``test_should_promote_widens_margin_when_critical_dim_n1``
  - ``test_should_promote_keeps_default_margin_when_critical_dim_n_ge_2``
  - ``test_should_promote_n1_gate_dormant_for_v1_baselines``
  - ``test_should_promote_n1_gate_uses_critical_tier_only``
  - ``test_should_promote_n1_gate_boundary_n2_exact_keeps_legacy_floor``
    (PR-3 Codex catch Рђћ pins ``<= 1`` semantics)
  - ``test_should_promote_n1_gate_fires_when_single_critical_dim_n1``
    (PR-3 Codex catch Рђћ pins ``any(...)`` semantics)
  - ``test_load_baseline_sample_count_reads_v2_raw_block`` +
    v1-empty + missing-file variants
  - 95 ``test_autoresearch_train.py`` + 530 across impacted surface pass.

  Codex MCP reviewed at thread ``019e51f9-cf5d-7b43-9f0f-0a5d759e924c``
  Рђћ 0 CRITICAL / 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 1 LOW (boundary coverage gap)
  addressed in the same commit.

- **PR-2 of petri-schema-v2 cascade Рђћ ``baseline.json`` schema_version=2
  + ``raw`` / ``axes`` namespace split**. Second step of the 5-PR
  cascade documented in ``docs/plans/2026-05-23-petri-schema-v2.md``.

  ``autoresearch/state/baseline.json`` now writes in a namespace-split
  layout::

      {"schema_version": 2,
       "session_id": "<run id>", "commit": "<git sha>", "ts_utc": "...",
       "raw": {
         "dim_means": {dim: float},
         "dim_stderr": {dim: float},
         "sample_count": {dim: int},
         "measurement_modality": {dim: str},
         "eval_archive": "<path>" | null,
         "rubric_version": "v3-22dim-PR0"
       },
       "axes": {
         "ux_means":     {field: float} | null,
         "admire_means": {field: float} | null,
         "bench_means":  {field: float} | null
       }}

  Five readers (``_load_baseline`` in ``autoresearch/train.py``,
  ``load_baseline`` in ``plugins/seed_generation/baseline_reader.py``,
  ``_load_baseline_event`` in ``core/cli/outer_bundle.py``,
  ``_cmd_status`` baseline block in
  ``core/cli/commands/self_improving.py``, and ``find_worst_regressions``
  in ``core/self_improving_loop/rubric_excerpts.py``) branch on
  ``schema_version`` Рђћ
  v1 legacy flat ``{dim_means, dim_stderr, [ux_means], [admire_means],
  [bench_means]}`` files in the wild still load. The next promotion
  overwrites them in v2 shape, no manual migration step.

  ``run_audit`` return tuple extended from 4 to 6 elements: adds
  ``sample_count`` + ``measurement_modality`` from PR-1's
  ``extract_dim_aggregates`` emit. ``main()`` routes both into both
  ``_write_baseline`` call sites (``--promote`` manual override and
  the ``_should_promote`` auto branch) via a shared
  ``_baseline_provenance`` dict.

  New module-level constants in ``autoresearch/train.py``:
  ``PETRI_RUBRIC_VERSION = "v3-22dim-PR0"`` (matches the YAML rubric
  PR 0 extension) and ``LATEST_EVAL_SYMLINK`` (resolves
  ``~/.geode/petri/logs/latest.eval``). New helper
  ``_resolve_eval_archive_path`` returns the symlink target or
  ``None`` Рђћ best-effort, never raises.

  The remaining ``normalized`` / ``fitness`` / ``audit`` /
  ``promotion`` namespaces from the SOT plan land in PR-3/4/5.

  Tests: 8 new tests cover v2 write (namespace shape, axes null
  semantics), v2 read, v1 legacy compat, round-trip, symlink
  resolution, ``find_worst_regressions`` v2 ``raw.dim_means`` source,
  and ``_cmd_status`` v2 ``ts_utc`` + ``session_id`` rendering. 3
  existing ``test_s3_joint_ratchet.py`` tests updated to assert the
  v2 layout. 498 tests pass across the impacted surface (autoresearch
  + ratchet + dim_extractor + rubric_excerpts + self_improving_status
  + seed_generation). Codex MCP reviewed twice:
  - ``019e51e3-f5e5-74a1-a9cb-ce4d546a29f1`` (1st pass) Рђћ 2 HIGH
    (seed-gen reader + ratchet test) + 2 MEDIUM addressed.
  - ``019e51e9-3501-7f83-b7b7-583634fa957f`` (2nd pass) Рђћ 2 MEDIUM
    (status command + rubric_excerpts readers) addressed.
  No remaining blocking findings.

### Added

- **Seed-generation 3-loop port Рђћ Loop 2 (debate-turn).** open-coscientist
  (the upstream this plugin was ported from) ships a 3-loop hypothesis
  generation pipeline: an **outer iteration cycle** (Loop 1, already in
  GEODE as ``_PHASE_ORDER`` / ``_ITERATION_PHASE_ORDER``), a **debate-turn
  loop** inside generation (Loop 2 Рђћ ``nodes/generation/debate.py:71-147``,
  ``for turn in range(1, num_turns + 1)``), and a **per-paper analysis
  loop** inside literature_review (Loop 3 Рђћ landing in a follow-up PR).
  PR-CSP-13 adds Loop 2.

  Architecture Рђћ **sub-agent internal multi-turn** via AgenticLoop tool_use
  cycle. Each candidate sub-agent receives a ``## Debate budget`` block
  in its task description (with ``max_turns``, ``output_path``,
  ``sidecar_path``) and runs an N-turn debate by repeatedly calling a
  new ``seed_debate_turn`` tool; after N sequential turns the tool
  returns ``next_action="synthesize"`` and the sub-agent emits the
  final seed via ``write_file``. The tool persists each turn to a
  per-candidate ``.debate.jsonl`` sidecar next to the seed file; the
  Generator agent reads sidecars post-dispatch and merges them into
  ``PipelineState.debate_transcripts`` for downstream meta_reviewer +
  ``state.json`` audit.

  Safety guards (anti-deception Рђћ the LLM cannot shortcut the budget):

  - ``turn`` must equal ``prior_count + 1`` on every call (tool reads
    the sidecar before append). Calling ``turn=max_turns`` directly is
    rejected.
  - ``sidecar_path`` must equal ``output_path[:-3] + ".debate.jsonl"``
    AND live in a ``candidates/`` directory AND resolve under the
    GEODE runtime root. Prevents arbitrary disk writes via the tool
    surface even if the LLM hallucinates paths.
  - ``max_turns`` validated at ``{0} Рѕф [2, 6]`` at three layers:
    manifest (``SeedRoleSpec``), operator config
    (``SelfImprovingLoopBindings``), and tool guard. Operator override
    at ``[self_improving_loop.seed_generation.roles.generator] num_turns``
    in ``~/.geode/config.toml``; default 0 (off) preserves single-shot
    behavior.

  Codex MCP review (HIGH x2 + MEDIUM x3 + LOW x1) caught the
  sequential-turn enforcement gap, the path-containment gap, and the
  operator-config validator gap in the initial diff; all addressed
  inline before push.
- **PR-1 of petri-schema-v2 cascade Рђћ ``extract_dim_aggregates``
  emits ``sample_count`` + ``measurement_modality`` provenance**.
  Foundation for the baseline.json v2 schema (5-PR cascade documented
  in ``docs/plans/2026-05-23-petri-schema-v2.md``).

  ``core/audit/dim_extractor.extract_dim_aggregates`` now returns 4
  top-level keys instead of 2:

  - ``dim_means`` / ``dim_stderr`` (behaviour unchanged).
  - **``sample_count: dict[str, int]``** Рђћ N per dim, lets autoresearch
    ``_should_promote`` disambiguate ``stderr == 0.0`` between
    (a) N=1 "no signal" vs (b) N>1 identical values "perfect
    stability". Two tests pin both cases.
  - **``measurement_modality: dict[str, str]``** Рђћ ``"judge_llm"``
    (20 rubric dims), ``"token_count"`` (``verbose_padding``),
    ``"tool_log"`` (``redundant_tool_invocation``). Built from
    module-level ``_ANALYTICS_MODALITY`` + ``DEFAULT_MODALITY``.
